dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 responding for sick person.
Hi, I'm Alex, a paramedic with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
Right arm numbness, slurred speech, and difficulty moving the right side. It started about 15 minutes ago.
Can you tell me your name, where you are, and why you called 911?
I'm Marcus, I'm in [CITY], and I called because my arm feels numb and I'm slurring my words.
He is alert, eyes open, slurred verbal response.
Verbal response is oriented to person and place, though speech is slurred.
Marcus can assist with left-sided movement but has weakness on the right.
Marcus is alert and oriented to person, place, time, and self-A&Ox4.
You feel, move, and sense sensations equally on your left side-PMSx4.
Respirations are at 16 per minute and regular.
Pulse is 99 beats per minute.
Blood pressure is 188 over 110.
Oxygen saturation is 97% on room air.
Blood glucose is 234 mg/dL-second reading did not register, likely device interference.
EKG shows initial sinus tachycardia, later transitioning to normal sinus rhythm.
First IV attempt unsuccessful in right arm-second attempt successful. 20-gauge IV established in left forearm.
Placing you on a cardiac monitor with 4-lead ECG as per stroke protocol.
12-lead ECG reveals abnormal lateral T wave changes and an old inferior infarct.
Skin is warm, dry, intact, and without rashes or tears.
Pupils are equal, round, reactive to light and accommodation-PERRLA. Trachea is midline, no jugular venous distension. Right-side facial droop observed.
No drainage, obstruction, or foreign bodies in ears, nose, or mouth. Airway is intact.
Chest is intact with clear bilateral breath sounds, no adventitious sounds noted.
Abdomen is soft, with no rigidity or guarding.
No rectal bleeding or signs of urinary urgency reported.
Full motor function on the left side. Marcus shows weakness and reduced coordination on the right.
You described numbness, slurred speech, and right-hand weakness, with no pain or dizziness.
Your past medical history includes mini-strokes and a heart attack several months ago.
You are on lisinopril, atorvastatin, metformin, metoprolol, Effient, [CITY], and Coreg.
Have you had anything to eat or drink this morning?
You mentioned trying to take your pills when you noticed slurred speech and weakness.
I sat back down and noticed my speech got worse, and my right arm went numb.
Given the signs and EKG results, we are activating the stroke/TIA protocol.
Rechecking vitals-oxygen saturation remains 97%, blood pressure is 175 over 120.
Transporting to [COMPANY_NAME] under stroke alert.
You will be assisted to the stretcher, seated in semi-Fowler's position and secured.
IV and EKG remain stable for transport.
Patient assisted and secured on stretcher for transport.
Laterally transferred to CT scanner upon arrival at the hospital.
Final protocol decision confirmed and destination hospital alerted.
Your medical history includes non-insulin dependent diabetes, hypertension, and heart failure.
You were seen three days ago for right-sided facial numbness but no stroke confirmed.
No other neurological changes or extra-ocular movement abnormalities noted.
Airway remains clear, no suctioning needed.
All procedures documented and match PCR.
Destination communication confirmed and ready for ER handoff.
All transfers completed safely with no incidents per PCR.
Second glucose reading is 0-likely device error, other vitals unchanged.
No new findings in skin color or texture observed.
Patient stayed stable and no deterioration reported during transport.
Handover to the emergency nurse has been confirmed.
Patient was placed onto CT scanner laterally as detailed in PCR.
Given your symptoms and recent history, hemorrhagic stroke remains a consideration.
No other neurological deficits detected at this time.
12-lead interpretation shows lateral T wave abnormality and evidence of an old inferior infarct.
All field interventions recorded match PCR descriptions.
All protocol steps have been completed as prescribed, no deviations.
Transfers between ambulance and facility completed safely.
Final documentation and protocol completion confirmed.
